tEn: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2, mostly written by Claude Fable
sEn: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2, mostly written by Claude Fable
absEn: Sponsored by: Atlassian — Give your agents a plan. Not a prompt. New Jira capabilities unlock full-context for AI-native software development. Assign tasks to Claude, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot, now directly from Jira. Learn more
absZh: 赞助方：Atlassian — 给你的智能体一个计划，而非提示。新的Jira功能为AI原生软件开发解锁了完整上下文。现在可以直接从Jira向Claude、Cursor或GitHub Copilot分配任务。了解更多

contrib.en: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2 introduces a new transaction model with comprehensive documentation.
contrib.zh: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2 引入了新的事务模型，并附有全面的文档。

contrib.en: Claude Fable (Anthropic's model) wrote most of the code, costing about $149.25 in API fees.
contrib.zh: Claude Fable（Anthropic 的模型）编写了大部分代码，API 费用约为 149.25 美元。

contrib.en: The release fixes 5 release-blocker bugs, including a critical issue with nested transactions.
contrib.zh: 该版本修复了 5 个发布阻塞错误，包括一个嵌套事务的关键问题。

contrib.en: Cross-model review (Claude reviewing GPT-5.5's work) uncovered two additional issues.
contrib.zh: 跨模型审查（Claude 审查 GPT-5.5 的工作）发现了另外两个问题。

contrib.en: Release notes were generated by the agent and deemed better than manual writing.
contrib.zh: 发布说明由智能体生成，被认为比手动编写更好。

contrib.en: The library now handles Python 3.12's autocommit setting without breaking tests.
contrib.zh: 该库现在能处理 Python 3.12 的自动提交设置，而不会破坏测试。

limits.en: The agent's work required 37 prompts and 34 commits, indicating high human oversight.
limits.zh: 智能体的工作需要 37 次提示和 34 次提交，表明需要大量人工监督。

limits.en: Cost ($149.25) may be prohibitive for smaller projects without subscription plans.
limits.zh: 成本（149.25 美元）对于没有订阅计划的小型项目可能过高。

limits.en: The model's availability is limited; Claude Fable will be removed from Max subscriptions.
limits.zh: 该模型的可用性有限；Claude Fable 将从 Max 订阅中移除。

limits.en: Cross-model review may not generalize to all software projects or bug types.
limits.zh: 跨模型审查可能无法推广到所有软件项目或错误类型。

limits.en: The release is a release candidate; stability in production is not yet confirmed.
limits.zh: 该版本是候选发布版；生产环境中的稳定性尚未确认。

paras.en: Sponsored by: Atlassian — Give your agents a plan. Not a prompt. New Jira capabilities unlock full-context for AI-native software development. Assign tasks to Claude, Cursor, or GitHub Copilot, now directly from Jira. Learn more
paras.zh: 赞助商：Atlassian — 给你的智能体一个计划，而非提示。新的 Jira 功能为 AI 原生软件开发解锁了完整上下文。现在可以直接从 Jira 分配任务给 Claude、Cursor 或 GitHub Copilot。了解更多

full.sec: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2, mostly written by Claude Fable (for about $149.25)
full.secZh: sqlite-utils 4.0rc2，主要由 Claude Fable 编写（花费约 149.25 美元）

paras.en: I wrote about the sqlite-utils 4.0rc1 release a couple of weeks ago. Since we only have Claude Fable on our Max subscriptions for a few more days, I decided to see if it could help me get to a 4.0 stable release that I felt truly comfortable about, since I try to keep to SemVer and like my incompatible major versions to be as rare as possible.
paras.zh: 几周前我写了关于 sqlite-utils 4.0rc1 版本的文章。由于我们的 Max 订阅中 Claude Fable 仅剩几天可用，我决定看看它能否帮助我达到一个真正让我满意的 4.0 稳定版本，因为我尽量遵循语义化版本控制，并且希望不兼容的主版本尽可能少。

paras.en: I started with this prompt, in Claude Code for web on my iPhone:
paras.zh: 我从这个提示开始，在 iPhone 上通过 Claude Code for web 操作：

paras.en: Here’s that initial report it created for me. There were some _significant_ problems that I hadn’t myself encountered yet—5 that Fable categorized as “release blockers”. Here’s the worst of the bunch:
paras.zh: 这是它为我创建的那份初始报告。其中有一些我自己尚未遇到的**重大**问题——Fable 将其中 5 个归类为“发布阻塞问题”。以下是其中最严重的一个：

paras.en: That’s a really bad bug! Very glad I didn’t ship that, although at least it would have been a bug I could fix in a 4.0.1 point release, not a design flaw that would force a 5.0.
paras.zh: 那真是一个严重的 bug！非常庆幸我没有发布它，尽管至少它是一个可以在 4.0.1 小版本中修复的 bug，而不是一个会迫使发布 5.0 的设计缺陷。

paras.en: Over the course of 37 prompts, 34 commits and +1,321 -190 code changes over 30 separate files, we worked through the entire set of feedback in turn, making several other design improvements along the way.
paras.zh: 在 37 个提示、34 次提交以及跨越 30 个文件的 +1,321 -190 代码变更过程中，我们依次处理了全部反馈，并沿途进行了若干其他设计改进。

paras.en: A weird thing about coding agents is that harder tasks like this one actually provide _more_ opportunity to do other things at the same time, since the agent sometimes needs 10-15 minutes to churn away on a new task. I went out to enjoy the Half Moon Bay 4th of July parade, occasionally checking in and prompting the next step for Fable from my phone.
paras.zh: 编码智能体的一件奇怪事情是，像这样更困难的任务实际上提供了**更多**同时做其他事情的机会，因为智能体有时需要 10-15 分钟来处理新任务。我出去享受了半月湾的 7 月 4 日游行，偶尔从手机上查看并提示 Fable 执行下一步。

paras.en: Full details in the PR and this shared transcript. I switched to my laptop for the final review, which I conducted through GitHub’s PR interface.
paras.zh: 完整细节见 PR 和这份共享记录。我切换到笔记本电脑进行最终审查，通过 GitHub 的 PR 界面进行。

paras.en: The most significant changes relate to transaction handling, which was the signature new feature in the earlier RC. The new RC now includes comprehensive documentation on the new transaction model, the intro to which I’ll quote here in full:
paras.zh: 最重要的变化与事务处理有关，这是之前 RC 版本中的标志性新特性。新的 RC 现在包含关于新事务模型的全面文档，我将在此处完整引用其引言：

paras.en: In reviewing Fable’s documentation—I find that reviewing the documentation edits first is an _excellent_ way to build an initial understanding of what has changed—I spotted this detail:
paras.zh: 在审查 Fable 的文档时——我发现首先审查文档编辑是构建对变更初步理解的**极好**方式——我注意到了这个细节：

paras.en: I admit I hadn’t thought about how sqlite-utils would react to the more recent autocommit setting, added in Python 3.12. It turns out “behave differently on those connections” equated to almost the entire test suite failing, so I worked with the model to ensure that this difference would not break how the library works.
paras.zh: 我承认我此前没有考虑过 sqlite-utils 如何应对 Python 3.12 中新增的较新自动提交设置。结果发现“在这些连接上表现不同”几乎等同于整个测试套件失败，因此我与模型合作，确保这种差异不会破坏库的工作方式。

full.sec: And a final review by GPT-5.5

paras.en: I used to think that the idea of having one model review the work of another was somewhat absurd—it felt weirdly superstitious. The problem is _it really does work_—I’ve started habitually having Anthropic’s best model review OpenAI’s work and vice versa, because I’ve had that turn up interesting results often enough to be valuable.
paras.zh: 我曾认为让一个模型审查另一个模型的工作有些荒谬——这感觉奇怪地像迷信。但问题在于它确实有效——我已经习惯性地让 Anthropic 的最佳模型审查 OpenAI 的工作，反之亦然，因为我发现这样做经常能产生有趣的结果，很有价值。

paras.en: I prompted Codex Desktop and GPT-5.5 xhigh with the following:
paras.zh: 我向 Codex Desktop 和 GPT-5.5 xhigh 输入了以下内容：

paras.en: Which was enough to turn up two issues worth investigating:
paras.zh: 这足以发现两个值得调查的问题：

paras.en: I pasted that into a fresh Fable session, which ran some experiments to confirm the problem:
paras.zh: 我将其粘贴到一个新的 Fable 会话中，该会话运行了一些实验来确认问题：

paras.en: Here’s the PR with the fix, and the full Claude Code transcript. Reviewing this code helped me build a better mental model of the edge cases of SQLite transaction semantics!
paras.zh: 这是包含修复的 PR 以及完整的 Claude Code 记录。审查这段代码帮助我更好地构建了 SQLite 事务语义边缘情况的思维模型！

full.secZh: 预估（无补贴）成本为 149.25 美元

paras.en: I upgraded to the Claude Max $200/month plan (I was previously on $100/month) to increase my Fable allowance for the remaining time until the July 7th Fablepocalypse, when even Claude Max subscribers will have to pay full API cost for the model.
paras.zh: 我将 Claude Max 订阅升级到了每月 200 美元的计划（之前是每月 100 美元），以增加我在 7 月 7 日 Fable 末日（届时连 Claude Max 订阅者也要为模型支付完整的 API 成本）之前剩余时间内的 Fable 配额。

paras.en: I was curious as to how much this would have cost me if I had been paying those costs directly. At first I thought those numbers weren’t available to me since I had run the work remotely using Claude Code for web, and then I realized I could run AgentsView inside that existing session to get that cost estimate!
paras.zh: 我很好奇，如果直接支付这些成本，我需要花多少钱。起初我以为我无法获得这些数字，因为我是通过 Claude Code for web 远程运行工作的，后来我意识到可以在现有会话中运行 AgentsView 来获取成本估算！

paras.en: Claude figured out how to use the session list --include-children command and came out with the following:
paras.zh: Claude 学会了如何使用 session list --include-children 命令，并得出了以下结果：

paras.en: I’m very glad I’m on that subscription! I really should have followed my own advice and leaned more heavily into subagents with cheaper models.
paras.zh: 我很庆幸自己订阅了这个计划！我真应该听从自己的建议，更多地依赖使用更便宜模型的子智能体。

paras.en: Here’s what claude.ai/settings/usage is showing me right now:
paras.zh: 以下是 claude.ai/settings/usage 目前向我显示的内容：

paras.en: I have several other major Fable-driven projects on the go right now as well, with the goal of hitting 100% on that Fable bar just in time for the price increase.
paras.zh: 我现在还在进行其他几个由 Fable 驱动的大型项目，目标是在价格上涨之前，让 Fable 进度条达到 100%。

full.sec: The full release notes for sqlite-utils 4.0rc2

paras.en: Here are the full release notes for the RC. I had Fable add these to an “Unreleased” section of the changelog as each change landed, reviewing them as it went. This has the neat side effect that the commit history of the changelog acts as a concise summary of each of the changes that went into the release.
paras.zh: 以下是 RC 版本的完整发布说明。我让 Fable 在每次变更落地时将其添加到变更日志的“未发布”部分，并随进展进行审查。这产生了一个简洁的副作用：变更日志的提交历史充当了发布中每个变更的简明摘要。

paras.en: In the past I’ve had a policy of writing release notes by hand, but honestly these are better than I would have created myself. Release notes are a great example of writing that I’m OK to outsource to agents because they need to be boring, predictable and accurate.
paras.zh: 过去，我坚持手动编写发布说明，但老实说，这些说明比我亲自编写的更好。发布说明是一个很好的例子，说明我乐意将写作外包给智能体，因为它们需要枯燥、可预测且准确。
